text: I feel a sense of quiet abandonment, as if the space itself is gently exhaling after the presence of people and conversation has receded. The glow of artificial lights reflects off glass and distant walls, casting an otherworldly atmosphere on what might usually be a vibrant hub of activity. In these subdued moments at the threshold of endings and beginnings, I am left to contemplate the emptiness—tinged both with melancholy and with peaceful solitude—left behind by human departure.
